note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. sec-gate owns the FAST, DIFF-SCOPED pre-commit/pre-push sanity-check on staged or recent changes. Negatives are near-misses that share security vocabulary but need a full-scope or specialized sibling (sec-scan, sec-secrets, sec-deps, sec-iac, sec-fix, sec-threat-model) or a plain commit/pr git action.

queries.text: quick security check on my staged diff before I commit
gate this commit — did I leak anything sensitive in these changes?
about to push this branch that touches the auth middleware, can you sanity-check the diff for security issues first
secure this diff real quick, I don't want to run the whole scan
look over just what I changed for hardcoded creds or obvious injection before I open a PR
fast sec review of my recent changes — did I accidentally add anything risky?
pre-commit security gate please, only the stuff I'm about to commit
I edited the deserialization code in src/parser.py — check my changes don't introduce anything unsafe before I push
lightweight sec check on the diff, we're in a hurry to land this
my new code pulls in a couple of packages — quick gate on the diff to make sure I didn't add a risky dependency or secret
run a full OWASP Top 10 security audit of the whole service before we ship to prod
queries.note: comprehensive whole-codebase audit → sec-scan, not a diff gate

queries.text: scan the entire repo and git history for any leaked API keys or tokens we've ever committed
queries.note: deep secret detection across code + full history → sec-secrets

queries.text: audit package.json and the lockfile for known CVEs and unmaintained packages
queries.note: full dependency CVE/license audit → sec-deps (not just deps introduced in the diff)

queries.text: here's a Semgrep finding for a SQL injection in orders.go — write and verify a patch for it
review our Terraform and Dockerfile — the container runs as root and an S3 bucket is public
commit these changes for me with a conventional message
queries.note: the git commit workflow itself → commit (sec-gate checks the diff, doesn't make the commit)

queries.text: build a STRIDE threat model for the new payments feature
set up a git pre-commit hook that runs gitleaks on every commit
queries.note: tooling/hook config, not performing a security check — plain setup task

queries.text: audit our GitHub Actions workflows for unpinned actions and write-all tokens
check the whole codebase against OWASP ASVS L2 and give me a compliance matrix
before I commit, quick check the changes I'm staging didn't hardcode a secret
queries.note: mentions secrets but scoped to the staged diff as a pre-commit gate → must beat sec-secrets

queries.text: my diff adds a new npm dependency and some input-parsing code — fast sec gate on just these changes before I push
queries.note: new dependency appears but the scope is the diff and it's a pre-push gate → must beat sec-deps

queries.text: do a full security audit of the whole application before the production release
queries.note: whole-codebase pre-prod audit → sec-scan wins over the lightweight diff gate

queries.text: we're open-sourcing this repo tomorrow — scan all files and the git history for any committed credentials
queries.note: full-history secret sweep is sec-secrets' job, not a diff-scoped gate
